revision.text:
[[ja:第六天古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.55027777777778|N|139.65991666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Kasedai Kofun Cluster|qid=Q65249392|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster}}}} {{Japanese Ancient Tomb | name = Dai-Rokuten Kofun | image = | part_of = {{ill|Kasedai Tomb Cluster|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249392}}
Hiyoshi–Kase Tomb Cluster | location = Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|qid=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki City|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|en|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|qid=Q1153579|lt=Saiwai Ward|1=Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki}} | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|33|1.0|N|139|39|35.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Dai-Rokuten Kofun|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Round Tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | map_size = | period = Late 6th Century | Historic Site = | notes = A tomb that once existed. | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture| coordinates = 35| coordinates = 33|coordinates = 01.0| lon_deg = 139| lon_min = 39| lon_sec = 35.7|map_dot_label = Dai-Rokuten Kofun|relief=1|position=bottom|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=22|width=250|caption=location within Kanagawa Prefecture|float=center|border=none|qq=}}{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|33|1.0|N|139|39|35.7|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|deity = {{ill|Omodaru and Ayakashikone|lt=Ayakashikone|lt=Ayakashikone|ja|阿夜訶志古泥神|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omodaru and Ayakashikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}}/{{ill|Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Sixth Heavenly Demon King|ja|天魔|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|1=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=Sixth Heavenly Demon King|lt_ja=第六天魔王|lt=Demon King of the Sixth Heaven (Dairokuten Mao)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|13=User:Immanuelle/Demon King of the Sixth Heaven|qq=}} }} '''Dairokuten Kofun''' (第六天古墳) was a tomb from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}) that once existed in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|qid=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki City|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}}, and {{ill|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|en|Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki|qid=Q1153579|lt=Saiwai Ward|1=Saiwai-ku, Kawasaki}}. Its tomb type was a {{ill|Round Tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it was one of the tombs of the {{ill|Kasedai Tomb Cluster|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasedai Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249392}} (or the Hiyoshi–Kase Tomb Cluster). ==Overview== In northern {{ill|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|en|Kawasaki, Kanagawa|qid=Q164234|lt=Kawasaki City|1=Kawasaki, Kanagawa}}, Kanagawa Prefecture, and northern {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City|1=Yokohama}}, a portion of the {{ill|Tama Hills|en|Tama Hills|qid=Q1502721|lt=Tama Hills|1=Tama Hills}} extends, typeing part of the {{ill|Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|ja|下末吉台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|1=Shimomatsuyoshi Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11360955}}—an area rising to about 35–40 meters in elevation between the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|qid=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}, {{ill|Yagami River|ja|矢上川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami River|1=Yagami River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583877}}, and {{ill|Tsurumi River|en|Tsurumi River|qid=Q7850292|lt=Tsurumi River|1=Tsurumi River}}. At its eastern edge lie plateaus known as “{{ill|Hiyoshidai Tomb Cluster|ja|日吉台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshidai Tomb Cluster|1=Hiyoshidai Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113285297}}”, represented by the linked plateau {{ill|Hiyoshidai|ja|日吉台 (横浜市)|lt=Hiyoshidai|lt_ja=日吉台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshidai|1=Hiyoshidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113119398}}, and “Yagamidai”, as well as “Kasedai”, where the Kasedai Tomb Cluster is situated ({{Sfn|松原|2006|qq=}}). Although Kasedai adjoins Hiyoshidai and Yagamidai to its east, a section of the {{ill|Yagami River|ja|矢上川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami River|1=Yagami River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583877}} flows between them, dividing the area ({{Sfn|安藤|2019|pp=8-9|qq=}}); the side on the Kasedai (east) belongs to Kawasaki City, while the Hiyoshi–Yagamidai (west) falls within Yokohama City. Despite being on separate plateaus, the Hiyoshidai Tomb Cluster and the Kasedai Tomb Cluster are regionally and chronologically close, and are sometimes collectively referred to as the '''Hiyoshi–Kase Tomb Cluster''' ({{Sfn|浜田|1998|p=7-12|qq=}}). ==Excavation Survey== {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|35|33|00.1|N|139|39|26.5|E|qq=}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 300 | height = 250 | caption = References {{Sfn|安藤|2019|p=11|qq=}}{{Sfn|安藤|2019|pp=171-173|qq=}}, and positional data restored from Kawasaki City’s administrative map service{{Cite web|Japanese-language book|author=Yokohama City Board of Education|url=https://www.city.kawasaki.jp/500/page/0000002354.html|title=Kawasaki City Urban Planning Intypeation Internet Service|work=Kawasaki City|accessdate=2022-07-31|qq=}}. | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord = {{Coord|35|33|01.0|N|139|39|35.7|E|qq=}} | map_dot_label = Dai-Rokuten Kofun | map_dot_label-pos = top | mark-title = Dai-Rokuten Kofun | map_dot_label-size = 11 | map_dot_label-color = dark gray | mark-size = 8 | mark = Red pog.svg | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|35|32|56.6|N|139|38|59.9|E|qq=}} | map_dot_label2 = Relocated Stone Chamber Site | map_dot_label-pos2 = top | mark-title2 = Relocated Stone Chamber Site | map_dot_label-size2 = 11 | map_dot_label-color2 = green | mark-size2 = 8 | mark2 = green pog.svg }} At one time, the western tip of Kasedai extended even further west than it does today, and on that plateau there existed both the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in Kawasaki City—namely, the {{ill|Hakusan Kofun|ja|白山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Kofun|qq=}} (with a total length of 87 meters)—and the round tomb, Dai-Rokuten Kofun (approximately 19 meters in diameter) (although these, along with the plateau itself, have since vanished due to earth removal). Both tombs were excavated in 1937 (Shōwa 12) by {{ill|Shibata Tsunehisa|ja|柴田常恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibata Tsunehisa|1=Jōe Shibata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17362654}}, Mori Sadanari ({{ill|Keio University|en|Keio University|qid=Q815432|lt=Keio University|1=Keio University}}), and the Mita Historical Society during an {{ill|Archaeological excavation|en|Archaeological excavation|qid=Q959782|lt=Excavation Survey|1=Archaeological excavation}} ({{Sfn|三田史学会|1953|pp=73-112|qq=}}{{Sfn|安藤|2019|p=7|qq=}}). ==Relocated Stone Chamber== After the survey, the tumulus of Dai-Rokuten Kofun, along with the {{ill|Hakusan Kofun|ja|白山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Kofun|qq=}}, was eliminated by earth removal work; however, the horizontally excavated stone chamber built with cut mudstone was relocated to the Keio University Hiyoshi Campus and displayed outdoors. Yet during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} and the subsequent campus {{ill|Seizure|ja|接収|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seizure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 収用|1=Expropriation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86757}} period by the {{ill|United States Armed Forces|en|United States Armed Forces|qid=Q11211|lt=U.S. Military|1=United States Armed Forces}} (1945–1949), it suffered severe damage, leaving only the stone material at its base intact ({{Sfn|安藤|2019|p=7|qq=}}{{Sfn|安藤|2019|pp=171-173|qq=}}). In addition, many of the grave goods excavated and once held by Keio University were lost to war damage during the Pacific War ({{Sfn|Kawasaki City Citizens' Museum|2006|p=90|qq=}}). Subsequently, in the 1970s, during the expansion of a high school sports ground, the stone chamber was re-relocated; however, because no records were kept at that time and the stone materials were soon reburied, its new location on campus became unknown and effectively lost ({{Sfn|安藤|2019|p=7|qq=}}). In 2012 (Heisei 24), Professor Hiromichi Ando of Keio University’s Faculty of Letters, while investigating the {{ill|Hiyoshidai Underground Bunker|ja|日吉台地下壕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshidai Underground Bunker|1=Hiyoshidai Underground Bunker|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85880645}} (a bulletproof vertical shaft) adjacent to a tennis court on the Hiyoshi Campus, discovered an outcrop of mudstone at the nearby surface. Recognizing it as the stone chamber of Dai-Rokuten Kofun, he conducted a survey from March 6 to 8, 2013 (Heisei 25) and identified the re-relocation site ({{Sfn|安藤|2019|p=7|qq=}}). == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kaseidai Kofun Cluster|ja|加瀬台古墳群|1=Kasedai Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaseidai Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249392}} * {{ill|Hakusan Kofun|ja|加瀬白山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山古墳}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi-Kase Kofun Cluster|ja|日吉台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi-Kase Kofun Cluster|1=Hiyoshidai Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113285297}} ==Footnotes== {{Footnote Help|qq=}} === notes === {{Reflist|group=notes|qq=}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2|qq=}} ==References== *{{Cite book|Japanese-language book|author=Mita Historical Society|title=Hiyoshi–Kase Tombs – Excavation Report of Hakusan Kofun and Dai-Rokuten Kofun-|year=1953|date=1953|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/103785|ncid=BN07524060|series=Archaeology and Ethnology Series|volume=2|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|Japanese-language book|editor=Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Association|title=Fifth Archaeological Lecture – Tombs of Kanagawa-|year=1998|date=1998-02-22|publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Association|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/19108|doi=10.24484/sitereports.19108|ref=harv|qq=}} **{{Cite book|Japanese-language book|first=Shinsuke|last=Hamada|chapter=Tombs of Kawasaki|title=Fifth Archaeological Lecture – Tombs of Kanagawa-|year=1998|date=1998-02-22|publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Association|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/19108|doi=10.24484/sitereports.19108|pages=7-12|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|Japanese-language book|author={{ill|Yokohama History Museum|en|Yokohama History Museum|qid=Q4902594|lt=Yokohama Museum of History|1=Yokohama History Museum}}|title=Ancient Tombs and burial goods of Yokohama|chapter=Kase Hakusan Kofun|page=9|publisher=Public Interest Incorporated Foundation {{ill|Yokohama City Furusato History Foundation|ja|横浜市ふるさと歴史財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Furusato History Foundation|1=Yokohama City Furusato History Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30267766}} {{ill|Yokohama City Buried Historic Site Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Buried Historic Site Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buried cultural property center|qid=Q11425913}}|year=2001|date=2001-01-27|ncid=BA52381705|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|Japanese-language book|author={{ill|Kawasaki City Citizens' Museum|ja|川崎市市民ミュージアム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Citizens' Museum|1=Kawasaki City Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11478412}}|title=The Emergence and Development of Tombs|chapter=Dai-Rokuten Kofun|page=90|series=Exhibition "Considering the Yayoi, Kofun, and Asuka builts"|publisher={{ill|Kawasaki City Citizens' Museum|ja|川崎市市民ミュージアム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki City Citizens' Museum|1=Kawasaki City Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11478412}}|year=2006|date=2006-04-29|ncid=BA77260278|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite Journal|Japanese-language journal|last=Matsubara|first=Akiko|journal=Keio University Hiyoshi Bulletin: Social Sciences|title=Topographical Changes of Keio University’s Hiyoshi & Yagami Campuses, and Shonan Fujisawa Campus|volume=16|publisher=Keio University Hiyoshi Bulletin Editorial Committee|year=2006|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN10425830-20060000-0001|date=2006|pages=1-13|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite Book|Japanese-language book|last=Ando|first=Hiromichi|title=Excavation Report of the Hiyoshidai Site Cluster: Research Results from 2006 to 2014-|publisher=Keio University Faculty of Letters, Department of Ethnology and Archaeology|year=2019|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=KO10007001-00000003-0001|date=2019-12-10|ref=harv|qq=}} **{{Cite Book|Japanese-language book|last=Ando|first=Hiromichi|chapter=Chapter 1: Background of the Investigation|title=Excavation Report of the Hiyoshidai Site Cluster: Research Results from 2006 to 2014-|publisher=Keio University Faculty of Letters, Department of Ethnology and Archaeology|year=2019|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=KO10007001-00000003-0001|date=2019-12-10|pp=1-7|ref=harv|qq=}} **{{Cite Book|Japanese-language book|last=Ando|first=Hiromichi|chapter=(3) Confirmation Survey of the Relocated Stone Chamber (K District)|title=Excavation Report of the Hiyoshidai Site Cluster: Research Results from 2006 to 2014-|publisher=Keio University Faculty of Letters, Department of Ethnology and Archaeology|year=2019|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=KO10007001-00000003-0001|date=2019-12-10|pp=171-173|ref=harv|qq=}} * {{Japanese History Stub|qq=}}
